description: A shellfish dish commonly prepared by steaming mussels until they open, often in broth made with wine, garlic, butter, herbs, or tomato.
preparation_steps: Clean and debeard the mussels and discard any that are damaged or remain open when tapped.
Add liquid such as wine, stock, or tomato base.
Add the mussels, cover, and steam until they open.
Discard unopened mussels and serve immediately with the cooking broth.

health_advice.benefits: Mussels provide protein, iron, vitamin B12, and other minerals.
health_advice.risks: Food safety matters because mussels are highly perishable and should be cooked properly. Rich sauces can add extra sodium and fat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose freshly sourced mussels and avoid overcooking them so the texture stays tender.

dietary_notes.note: Mussels are shellfish and naturally gluten-free, but bread or flour-thickened sauces can add gluten.

allergens: Possible dairy if butter or cream is used
Possible gluten if served with bread or thickened sauce
